title: Roli AI Music Coach Uses Conversational AI To Help You Learn Piano

Innovation
Consumer Tech
Roli AI Music Coach Uses Conversational AI To Help You Learn Piano
By
Anthony Karcz
,
Senior Contributor.

Follow Author

Roli's AI Music Coach can watch your hands in real-time
Roli
Roli’s in-app AI assistant has been around since last year, but it’s been a bit of a novelty, helping you find your way around the vast Roli library. Now, Roli has made AI an integral part of the mission with the launch of the Roli AI Music Coach.
What is Roli?
If you've ever expressed any interest in piano music (or fit the right demographic), you've seen the company's social media ads, with the keyboard with the light-up keys that pairs with an app that you can use to learn songs
Guitar Hero
style, hitting the notes as they cascade across the screen.
It's a fun, casual way to start learning how to play (or pick up an old habit back up) and it's aided by a myriad of lessons with professional instructors who walk you through songs, step-by-step, altering the tempo so that you can practice and offering tips on things like finger-placement.
Speaking of fingers, Roli launched a next-generation element to their ecosystem last year. After the acquisition of UltraLeap, a company who specialized in hand tracking, Roli was able to accelerate development of Airwave. It looks like a tablet stand but it's really a fully integrated infrared camera system that tracks the 27 joints in your hands. It gives you a representation of your hands on the screen so that you can easily keep an eye on your form and finger placement.
But its purpose is fully realized with the launch of the AI Music Coach.
MORE FOR YOU
Roli AI Music Coach — Your Favorite Piano Teacher, Without the Posture Corrections
The pre-recorded piano teachers in the Roli app are engaging and clearly know their craft…but you're still "running on rails," to borrow a gaming term. Anyone who's had in-person piano instruction will likely feel like they're cheating as they slouch over the keyboard and let their wrists rest on the tabletop.
Now, the AI Music Coach won't judge your curved spine or floppy wrists but it
will
use the input from Airwave to see what fingers you're using and offer advice on placement and form while you work through a song.
But I'm getting ahead of myself, how do you get to that point in the first place?
Roli's AI Music Coach will suggest songs based on your interests, skill level, and experience.
Roli
AI Music Coach uses a conversational interface to conduct a quick interview, determining how much experience you have and what kind of music you like (or want to play) and adjusting the lesson accordingly. There's permanence between sessions as well so you don't have to start from scratch every time. It stays in tune with your ability as well. As you progress, AI Music Coach will adjust your lessons accordingly so that it's meeting your needs.
Roli's AI Music Coach is multi-lingual as well. It currently offers instruction in 40 languages, ensuring that there isn't a language barrier between you and learning how to play.
The app is still growing as Roli conducts their closed beta (more on that in a second). Right now it has a tendency to lock you into the first section of a song, rather than advancing you to the next section once you've mastered the current one. But the potential is there, especially when it comes to discovering songs that meet your skill level. I certainly don't know any modern classical music, but I was thrilled to try and learn some "new to me" music that the AI Music Coach thought would be good for me.
Roli AI Music Coach gives you feedback as you practice.
Roli
As you practice a song, the app uses Airwave to correct fingering mistakes, pausing the lesson to let you know what finger you should be using for which note. Oddly, the fingering coach and the music coach use different voices but it's all definitely the same bot as the coach will give you feedback after a run, recommending things like slowing down the tempo so that you can work on the notes giving you problems.
As someone who spent years taking piano lessons in their youth, the "all encouragement, no discouragement" approach is slightly disconcerting and I keep chiding myself when I notice that I'm not using proper form…but that's the point. Just the idea of finding someone to teach you how to play an instrument is scary, because we've all heard the stories of terrible coaches. Roli's mission of "Free the Music" means easing the path to playing music and making it fun.
Roland Lamb, co-founder and CEO of ROLI had this to say about the launch: “The combination of hand tracking, advanced voice capabilities, and deep musical knowledge, results in an entirely new type of practice, with a teacher or for standalone practice. It’s more fun, but also more effective, because the AI Coach can give you holistic feedback on your session. You don’t need to be able to read music to learn, you don’t even need to be able to read."
And for everyone that gets a bit wary when AI is touted as being "more effective," take heart. The company's manifesto on AI learning (which you can access from the
Roli AI Music Coach site
) makes it clear that they see AI as a tool to enhance human creativity, not a replacement for humans.
How Do I Get The Roli AI Music Coach?
If you have an Airwave, then you're in luck — you're already part of the closed beta that starts today. As pre-orders for the Airwave are filled (they're currently backlogged for a bit as production catches up with demand), they'll be rolled into the beta as well.
A public beta starts at the end of March, but Roli hasn't yet disclosed if there will be usage limits (the bailiwick of many an AI feature). And if you prefer the video piano lessons, don't worry, they're not going away, though it's clear from the press release that Roli sees the AI Music Coach to be the new "best use case" for their ecosystem.
Take that, guitarists.
Roli
And there’s more to the Roli app than just the AI Music Coach — in addition to video lessons, there’s a vast song library that you can explore and learn, games that you can play, even a free mode where you can just noodle around on the keys. You can even have the Roli app just display sheet music and take the traditional approach.
There are several packages available for purchase on the Roli
AI Music Coach site
, from a full to mini keyboard experience, all with Airwave, and starting around $500.
